dag.dag_name: Store Sales POS heartbeat retry storm

tasks.dependencies: Fetches the list of Marketing endpoints/accounts/vendors to map over

tasks.task_type: PythonOperator.expand (dynamic task mapping, exponential backoff retries)
tasks.dependencies: One mapped instance per endpoint; retry policy is error-code-aware
Aggregates success/failure/retry counts across mapped instances
Evaluates aggregate retry/error rate against a storm threshold
Branch: retry rate within normal bounds
Branch: retry storm detected, trips circuit breaker for downstream calls
Pages on-call, attaches AutoRCA retry-storm analysis
Loads Marketing target with successfully retrieved records

operator_config.sql: SELECT 1 AS autorca_probe, 'RTL_MKT_RETRY' AS dag_id, 'load_target' AS task_name;

incident.incident_name: Store Sales POS heartbeat retry storm
incident.description: Retry Storm incident on the Marketing pipeline (RTL_MKT_RETRY): store Sales POS heartbeat retry storm.
incident.symptoms: POS terminal heartbeat validation task, dynamically mapped per store (450 stores), generated 5,000+ retries after a regional network outage.
incident.root_cause: A regional ISP outage affected 60 stores simultaneously, and the DAG's per-store retry logic had no outage-awareness, so it kept retrying unreachable stores at the same aggressive interval as healthy ones, wasting scheduler/worker capacity that could have served healthy-store tasks faster.
incident.business_impact: Sales rollup for all 450 stores delayed, not just the 60 affected by the outage, because the retry storm consumed the shared worker pool.
incident.resolution: Add a circuit breaker per store that backs off aggressively (and isolates) after 3 consecutive failures, freeing pool capacity for healthy stores to complete on time.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: A regional ISP outage affected 60 stores simultaneously, and the DAG's per-store retry logic had no outage-awareness, so it kept retrying unreachable stores at the same aggressive interval as healthy ones, wasting scheduler/worker capacity that could have served healthy-store tasks faster.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow shows 60 specific store-mapped task instances consuming disproportionate scheduler time relative to their eventual failure outcome
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog network.region_outage_flag (from an ISP status integration) correlates with the spike in POS heartbeat retries
expected_rca.resolution: Add a circuit breaker per store that backs off aggressively (and isolates) after 3 consecutive failures, freeing pool capacity for healthy stores to complete on time.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Add store/vendor/site health-state tracking to the metadata model so the DAG factory can generate outage-aware, isolated retry logic per mapped instance rather than uniform retry policy.
